title: [TEST COPY] BNCI 2015-003 P300 dataset
description: An auditory brain-computer interface dataset implementing the AMUSE (Auditory Multi-class Spatial ERP) paradigm for spelling applications. The dataset comprises EEG recordings from 10 healthy subjects performing an auditory oddball task with spatial cues from six speaker locations, achieving online spelling performance up to 1.41 characters per minute. Data include preprocessed EEG signals with event-related potentials (P300, N200) annotated according to HED 8.4.0 standards.
methods_description: EEG acquisition at 256 Hz from 8 channels (Fz, Cz, P3, Pz, P4, PO7, Oz, PO8) using Ag/AgCl electrodes with nose reference. Stimuli presented from six speakers at ear height (60° spacing, 65 cm radius) with stimulus onset asynchrony of 175 ms. Online preprocessing included hardware analog band-pass filtering (0.1-250 Hz), low-pass filtering (40 Hz), and downsampling to 100 Hz. Spatio-temporal feature extraction using r2 coefficient with linear discriminant analysis classifier and Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage regularization.

funding_references.funder_name: FP7-ICT PASCAL2 Network of Excellence ICT-216886
